'''天日鷲神'''（アメノヒワシノカミ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。 {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 天日鷲神 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 世代名 = | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 天日鷲神（アメノヒワシノカミ） | 別名 = 天日鷲命、天日鷲翔矢命、天毘和志可気流夜命、日鷲命、天日和志命、天比和志命など | 別称 = <!-- 天孫、山幸彦など神名でない称号 -->麻植神、忌部神 | 神階 = なし | 神格 = [[鳥]]神、[[機織]]神、[[紡績]]神 | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]] * [[忌部神社]] 等 }} | 御利益 = <!-- 五穀豊穣、安産、学問など --> | 記紀等 = [[日本書紀]]、[[古語拾遺]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[忌部氏|阿波忌部]]・安房忌部、神麻績連、天語連、長幡部連、[[倭文氏|倭文連]]、田辺宿禰、竹原氏、巨椋連、大椋置始連、今木連、[[弓削氏|弓削連]]、多米連、[[粟国造]]、秋月直、美努連（三野県主）、鳥取部連、神宮部造、[[県犬養氏|県犬養連]] }} == 概要 == 『日本書紀』や『古語拾遺』に登場する。[[阿波国]]を開拓し、穀麻を植えて紡績の業を創始した阿波（あわ）の[[忌部氏]]（いんべし）の祖神。
'''麻植神'''（おえのかみ）、'''忌部神'''（いんべのかみ）とも呼ばれる<ref name="週刊神社"/>。また[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]または[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]の裔神の'''天日鷲翔矢命'''（あめのひわしかけるやのみこと）ともされる。 == 記述 == [[神話]]で知られているのは[[天照大神]]が[[天岩戸]]に入られたとき、岩戸の前で神々の[[踊り]]が始まり、天日鷲神が[[弦楽器]]を奏でると、弦の先に[[鷲]]が止まった。多くの神々が、これは世の中を明るくする吉祥（きっしょう）を表す鳥といって喜ばれ、この神の名として鷲の字を加えて、天日鷲命とされた。という内容である<ref name="週刊神社">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>。後に[[平田篤胤]]は、神武天皇の戦の勝利に貢献した鳥と同一だと言及している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。
『[[日本書紀]]』では天の岩戸の一書に「[[粟国造|粟（あわ）の国]]の忌部の遠祖天日鷲命の作る[[木綿 (ゆう)]]を用い」とある。
『[[古語拾遺]]』によると、天日鷲神は[[フトダマ|太玉命]]に従う四柱の神のうちの1柱である。やはり、[[天照大神]]が天岩戸に隠れた際に、穀（[[カジノキ]]：[[コウゾ|楮]]の一種）・木綿などを植えて白和幣（にきて）を作ったとされる。そのため、天日鷲神は「[[麻植郡|麻植]]（おえ）の神」とも呼ばれ、[[紡績業]]・[[製紙業]]の神となる。また、[[天富命]]は天日鷲神の孫を率いて粟国へと行き、穀・[[アサ|麻]]を植えた。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[国造本紀]]」には、[[神武天皇]]の御世に天日鷲命を[[伊勢国造]]に定めたと伝わる。一方、『[[伊勢国風土記]]』逸文には、[[神武天皇]]が[[天日別命]]を[[伊勢国]]に遣わし、[[伊勢津彦]]を平定したとされている。
また、天日鷲神は一般に[[お酉様]]として知られ、[[豊漁]]、[[商工業]]繁栄、[[開運]]、[[開拓]]、殖産の守護神として[[信仰]]されている。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、[[県犬養氏]]や天語連の祖とされる。 == 祀る神社 == * 鷲神社（[[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]） - 主祭神 * 忌部神社（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]二軒屋町） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|[[天皇]]即位の[[大嘗祭]]に際して、阿波忌部の末裔とされる[[三木氏#阿波三木氏|三木家]]が育てた麻を元に、麁服（あらたえ）を調進している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <references/> == 関連項目 == * [[酉の市]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/yuisyo.html 酉の市とは 御由緒- 浅草 鷲神社公式ホームページ] * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=45 忌部神社]（徳島県神社庁） {{日本神話}}{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あめのひわしのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:商業神]] [[Category:忌部氏|神あめのひわしのかみ]]

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文氏}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神[[ja:天日鷲神]]) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|translation=Takami no Mikoto}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文氏}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神[[ja:天日鷲神]]) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|translation=Takami no Mikoto}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神[[ja:天日鷲神]]) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|translation=Takami no Mikoto}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.

Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文氏}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神[[ja:天日鷲神]]) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文氏}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神[[ja:天日鷲神]]) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文氏}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神[[ja:天日鷲神]]) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=draft}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|12=draft}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|12=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|12=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 天日鷲神 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 世代名 = | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 天日鷲神（アメノヒワシノカミ） | 別名 = 天日鷲命、天日鷲翔矢命、天毘和志可気流夜命、日鷲命、天日和志命、天比和志命など | 別称 = <!-- 天孫、山幸彦など神名でない称号 -->麻植神、忌部神 | 神階 = なし | 神格 = [[鳥]]神、[[機織]]神、[[紡績]]神 | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]] * [[忌部神社]] 等 }} | 御利益 = <!-- 五穀豊穣、安産、学問など --> | 記紀等 = [[日本書紀]]、[[古語拾遺]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[忌部氏|阿波忌部]]・安房忌部、神麻績連、天語連、長幡部連、[[倭文氏|倭文連]]、田辺宿禰、竹原氏、巨椋連、大椋置始連、今木連、[[弓削氏|弓削連]]、多米連、[[粟国造]]、秋月直、美努連（三野県主）、鳥取部連、神宮部造、[[県犬養氏|県犬養連]] }} '''天日鷲神'''（アメノヒワシノカミ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 『日本書紀』や『古語拾遺』に登場する。[[阿波国]]を開拓し、穀麻を植えて紡績の業を創始した阿波の[[忌部氏]]の祖神。
'''麻植神'''（おえのかみ）、'''忌部神'''（いんべのかみ）とも呼ばれる<ref name="週刊神社"/>。また[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]または[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]の裔神の'''天日鷲翔矢命'''（あめのひわしかけるやのみこと）ともされる。
== 記述 == [[神話]]で知られているのは[[天照大神]]が[[天岩戸]]に入られたとき、岩戸の前で神々の[[踊り]]が始まり、天日鷲神が[[弦楽器]]を奏でると、弦の先に[[鷲]]が止まった。多くの神々が、これは世の中を明るくする吉祥を表す鳥といって喜ばれ、この神の名として鷲の字を加えて、天日鷲命とされた。という内容である<ref name="週刊神社">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社|publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>。後に[[平田篤胤]]は、神武天皇の戦の勝利に貢献した鳥と同一だと言及している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。
『[[日本書紀]]』では天の岩戸の一書に「[[粟国造|粟の国]]の忌部の遠祖天日鷲命の作る[[木綿 (ゆう)|木綿]]を用い」とある。
『[[古語拾遺]]』によると、天日鷲神は[[フトダマ|太玉命]]に従う四柱の神のうちの1柱である。やはり、[[天照大神]]が天岩戸に隠れた際に、穀（[[カジノキ]]：[[コウゾ|楮]]の一種）・木綿などを植えて白和幣（にきて）を作ったとされる。そのため、天日鷲神は「[[麻植郡|麻植]]の神」とも呼ばれ、[[紡績業]]・[[製紙業]]の神となる。また、[[天富命]]は天日鷲神の孫を率いて粟国へと行き、穀・[[アサ|麻]]を植えた。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[国造本紀]]」には、[[神武天皇]]の御世に天日鷲命を[[伊勢国造]]に定めたと伝わる。一方、『[[伊勢国風土記]]』逸文には、[[神武天皇]]が[[天日別命]]を[[伊勢国]]に遣わし、[[伊勢津彦]]を平定したとされている。
また、天日鷲神は一般に[[お酉様]]として知られ、[[豊漁]]、[[商工業]]繁栄、[[開運]]、[[開拓]]、殖産の守護神として[[信仰]]されている。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、[[県犬養氏]]や天語連の祖とされる。
== 祀る神社 == * 鷲神社（[[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]） - 主祭神 * 忌部神社（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]二軒屋町） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|[[天皇]]即位の[[大嘗祭]]に際して、阿波忌部の末裔とされる[[三木氏#阿波三木氏|三木家]]が育てた麻を元に、麁服（あらたえ）を調進している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|12=draft}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績|12=draft}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=draft}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|12=draft}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=draft}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god|12=draft}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|12=draft}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=draft}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|12=draft}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|12=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|12=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|12=draft}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績|12=draft}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=draft}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|12=draft}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=draft}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god|12=draft}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|12=draft}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=draft}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|12=draft}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|12=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|12=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|12=draft}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=draft}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|12=draft}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|12=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|12=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|12=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|12=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|12=draft}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績|12=draft}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=draft}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=draft}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|12=draft}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=draft}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god|12=draft}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|12=draft}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=draft}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|12=draft}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|12=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|12=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 天日鷲神 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 世代名 = | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 天日鷲神（アメノヒワシノカミ） | 別名 = 天日鷲命、天日鷲翔矢命、天毘和志可気流夜命、日鷲命、天日和志命、天比和志命など | 別称 = <!-- 天孫、山幸彦など神名でない称号 -->麻植神、忌部神 | 神階 = なし | 神格 = [[鳥]]神、[[機織]]神、[[紡績]]神 | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]] * [[忌部神社]] 等 }} | 御利益 = <!-- 五穀豊穣、安産、学問など --> | 記紀等 = [[日本書紀]]、[[古語拾遺]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[忌部氏|阿波忌部]]・安房忌部、神麻績連、天語連、長幡部連、[[倭文氏|倭文連]]、田辺宿禰、竹原氏、巨椋連、大椋置始連、今木連、[[弓削氏|弓削連]]、多米連、[[粟国造]]、秋月直、美努連（三野県主）、鳥取部連、神宮部造、[[県犬養氏|県犬養連]] }} '''天日鷲神'''（アメノヒワシノカミ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 『日本書紀』や『古語拾遺』に登場する。[[阿波国]]を開拓し、穀麻を植えて紡績の業を創始した阿波の[[忌部氏]]の祖神。
'''麻植神'''（おえのかみ）、'''忌部神'''（いんべのかみ）とも呼ばれる<ref name="週刊神社"/>。また[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]または[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]の裔神の'''天日鷲翔矢命'''（あめのひわしかけるやのみこと）ともされる。
== 記述 == [[神話]]で知られているのは[[天照大神]]が[[天岩戸]]に入られたとき、岩戸の前で神々の[[踊り]]が始まり、天日鷲神が[[弦楽器]]を奏でると、弦の先に[[鷲]]が止まった。多くの神々が、これは世の中を明るくする吉祥を表す鳥といって喜ばれ、この神の名として鷲の字を加えて、天日鷲命とされた。という内容である<ref name="週刊神社">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社|publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>。後に[[平田篤胤]]は、神武天皇の戦の勝利に貢献した鳥と同一だと言及している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。
『[[日本書紀]]』では天の岩戸の一書に「[[粟国造|粟の国]]の忌部の遠祖天日鷲命の作る[[木綿 (ゆう)|木綿]]を用い」とある。
『[[古語拾遺]]』によると、天日鷲神は[[フトダマ|太玉命]]に従う四柱の神のうちの1柱である。やはり、[[天照大神]]が天岩戸に隠れた際に、穀（[[カジノキ]]：[[コウゾ|楮]]の一種）・木綿などを植えて白和幣（にきて）を作ったとされる。そのため、天日鷲神は「[[麻植郡|麻植]]の神」とも呼ばれ、[[紡績業]]・[[製紙業]]の神となる。また、[[天富命]]は天日鷲神の孫を率いて粟国へと行き、穀・[[アサ|麻]]を植えた。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[国造本紀]]」には、[[神武天皇]]の御世に天日鷲命を[[伊勢国造]]に定めたと伝わる。一方、『[[伊勢国風土記]]』逸文には、[[神武天皇]]が[[天日別命]]を[[伊勢国]]に遣わし、[[伊勢津彦]]を平定したとされている。
また、天日鷲神は一般に[[お酉様]]として知られ、[[豊漁]]、[[商工業]]繁栄、[[開運]]、[[開拓]]、殖産の守護神として[[信仰]]されている。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、[[県犬養氏]]や天語連の祖とされる。
== 祀る神社 == * 鷲神社（[[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]） - 主祭神 * 忌部神社（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]二軒屋町） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|[[天皇]]即位の[[大嘗祭]]に際して、阿波忌部の末裔とされる[[三木氏#阿波三木氏|三木家]]が育てた麻を元に、麁服（あらたえ）を調進している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。}}
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[酉の市]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/yuisyo.html 酉の市とは 御由緒 - 浅草 鷲神社公式ホームページ] * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=45 忌部神社]（徳島県神社庁）

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[天日鷲神]] to [[Otori-sama]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 天日鷲神 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 世代名 = | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = [[天津神]] | 全名 = 天日鷲神（アメノヒワシノカミ） | 別名 = 天日鷲命、天日鷲翔矢命、天毘和志可気流夜命、日鷲命、天日和志命、天比和志命など | 別称 = <!-- 天孫、山幸彦など神名でない称号 -->麻植神、忌部神 | 神階 = なし | 神格 = [[鳥]]神、[[機織]]神、[[紡績]]神 | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 神社 = {{Plainlist| * [[鷲神社 (台東区)|鷲神社]] * [[忌部神社]] 等 }} | 御利益 = <!-- 五穀豊穣、安産、学問など --> | 記紀等 = [[日本書紀]]、[[古語拾遺]] 等 | 関連氏族 = [[忌部氏|阿波忌部]]・安房忌部、神麻績連、天語連、長幡部連、[[倭文氏|倭文連]]、田辺宿禰、竹原氏、巨椋連、大椋置始連、今木連、[[弓削氏|弓削連]]、多米連、[[粟国造]]、秋月直、美努連（三野県主）、鳥取部連、神宮部造、[[県犬養氏|県犬養連]] }} '''天日鷲神'''（アメノヒワシノカミ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 『日本書紀』や『古語拾遺』に登場する。[[阿波国]]を開拓し、穀麻を植えて紡績の業を創始した阿波の[[忌部氏]]の祖神。
'''麻植神'''（おえのかみ）、'''忌部神'''（いんべのかみ）とも呼ばれる<ref name="週刊神社"/>。また[[タカミムスビ|高魂命]]または[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]の裔神の'''天日鷲翔矢命'''（あめのひわしかけるやのみこと）ともされる。
== 記述 == [[神話]]で知られているのは[[天照大神]]が[[天岩戸]]に入られたとき、岩戸の前で神々の[[踊り]]が始まり、天日鷲神が[[弦楽器]]を奏でると、弦の先に[[鷲]]が止まった。多くの神々が、これは世の中を明るくする吉祥を表す鳥といって喜ばれ、この神の名として鷲の字を加えて、天日鷲命とされた。という内容である<ref name="週刊神社">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社|publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>。後に[[平田篤胤]]は、神武天皇の戦の勝利に貢献した鳥と同一だと言及している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。
『[[日本書紀]]』では天の岩戸の一書に「[[粟国造|粟の国]]の忌部の遠祖天日鷲命の作る[[木綿 (ゆう)|木綿]]を用い」とある。
『[[古語拾遺]]』によると、天日鷲神は[[フトダマ|太玉命]]に従う四柱の神のうちの1柱である。やはり、[[天照大神]]が天岩戸に隠れた際に、穀（[[カジノキ]]：[[コウゾ|楮]]の一種）・木綿などを植えて白和幣（にきて）を作ったとされる。そのため、天日鷲神は「[[麻植郡|麻植]]の神」とも呼ばれ、[[紡績業]]・[[製紙業]]の神となる。また、[[天富命]]は天日鷲神の孫を率いて粟国へと行き、穀・[[アサ|麻]]を植えた。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「[[国造本紀]]」には、[[神武天皇]]の御世に天日鷲命を[[伊勢国造]]に定めたと伝わる。一方、『[[伊勢国風土記]]』逸文には、[[神武天皇]]が[[天日別命]]を[[伊勢国]]に遣わし、[[伊勢津彦]]を平定したとされている。
また、天日鷲神は一般に[[お酉様]]として知られ、[[豊漁]]、[[商工業]]繁栄、[[開運]]、[[開拓]]、殖産の守護神として[[信仰]]されている。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』によれば、[[県犬養氏]]や天語連の祖とされる。
== 祀る神社 == * 鷲神社（[[東京都]][[台東区]][[千束]]） - 主祭神 * 忌部神社（[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]二軒屋町） - 主祭神{{Unbulleted list|[[天皇]]即位の[[大嘗祭]]に際して、阿波忌部の末裔とされる[[三木氏#阿波三木氏|三木家]]が育てた麻を元に、麁服（あらたえ）を調進している<ref name="週刊神社"/>。}}
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[酉の市]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://otorisama.or.jp/yuisyo.html 酉の市とは 御由緒 - 浅草 鷲神社公式ホームページ] * [http://awa-jinjacho.jp/shrine/detail.html?id=45 忌部神社]（徳島県神社庁）

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|qq=draft}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | Rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績|qq=draft}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|qq=draft}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|qq=draft}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god|qq=draft}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|qq=draft}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|qq=draft}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|qq=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|qq=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.
== References == <references/>

The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft}}. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|qq=draft}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|qq=draft}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|qq=draft}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amanotomohime|ja|天富命|translation=Amanotomohime|qq=draft}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|qq=draft}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.
== References == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|qq=draft}}

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe clan|qq=}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}.

When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Atsutane|qq=}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.

Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. {{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly deities|qq=}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | Rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weaving|qq=}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spinning|qq=}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe clan|qq=}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateru clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|deity|ja|神 (神道)|translation=god|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/deity|qq=}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe clan|qq=}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takami Musubi|ja|タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi|translation=Takami no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} or {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|translation=Kamimusuhi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|qq=}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Atsutane|qq=}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/asa|qq=}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shojiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.
== References == <references/>

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly deities|qq=}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | Rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weaving|qq=}}, {{ill|Spinning|ja|紡績|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spinning|qq=}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe clan|qq=}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateru clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}} }}
