== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} entered the {{ill|Amanoiwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|qq=}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Atsutane|qq=}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}}."
According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古語拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|translation=Futodama no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|asa|ja|アサ|translation=hemp|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/asa|qq=}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|ja|忌部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbe Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shojiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shojiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|qq=|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

(Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.
== References == <references/>

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.
== References == <references/>
== See Also == * [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] * {{ill|Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)|ja|鷲神社 (台東区)|1=Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|lt=Ōtori Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine (Taito Ward)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine (Taito Ward)|qq=}} * {{ill|Torinoichi|ja|酉の市|1=Tori-no-Ichi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinoichi|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori-no-Ichi|qq=}} * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]]

Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

{{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652387}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a [[:en:Kami|Deity]] appearing in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. The deity appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]]. The ancestor deity of the [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe clan]] (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of [[:en:Takamimusubi|Takamimusubi]] or [[:en:Kamimusubi|Kamimusubi]].
== Overview == According to the mythology, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] entered the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]], the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kojiki]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed [[:en:Futodama|Futodama]]. Also, when [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and [[:en:Cannabis|asa]].
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at [[:en:Inbe Shrine|Inbe Shrine]] and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} or {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}}.

When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.

According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|en|Futodama|WD=Q11059539|lt=Futodama}}. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=asa}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.

Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|WD=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|WD=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

{{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | Rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weaving|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 織機|1=loom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q173056}}, {{ill|Spinning (textiles)|en|Spinning (textiles)|WD=Q59172|lt=Spinning}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateru clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652387}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} or {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} entered the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|en|Amano-Iwato|WD=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|en|Futodama|WD=Q11059539|lt=Futodama}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=asa}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|WD=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.

'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} or {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}}.

Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|WD=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. {{Infobox deity | name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity = {{ill|Heavenly deities|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Amanohiwashi no Kami | other_names = Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto, Amanobiwashikakeryuya no Mikoto, Hiwashi no Mikoto, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto, Amanobiwashi no Mikoto, etc. | alias = Moegami, Imbe no Kami | Rank = None | divine_role = {{ill|Weaving|ja|機織|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Weaving|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 織機|1=loom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q173056}}, {{ill|Spinning (textiles)|en|Spinning (textiles)|WD=Q59172|lt=Spinning}} | father = | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | children = | shrine = | benefits = | records = | related_clans = {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}} (Awa Inbe)・Awa Inbe, Kamiashikyu no Muraji, Amakoto no Muraji, Nagahata no Muraji, {{ill|Shitateru clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateru clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, Tanabe no Sukune, Takehara clan, Okura no Muraji, Okura no Oshihajime no Muraji, Imaki no Muraji, {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}, Tome no Muraji, {{ill|Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|粟国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Awa Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652387}}, Akizuki no Atai, Minu no Muraji (Mikawa no Kuni no Aruji), Tottori no Muraji, Jingunobe no Miyatsuko, {{ill|Inukai clan|ja|県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukai clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai clan|qq=}} }}
'''Amanohiwashi no Kami''' (天日鷲神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|WD=Q524158|lt=Deity}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. The deity appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}. The ancestor deity of the {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe clan}} (inbe) of Awa, who developed the Awa region and initiated the spinning industry by planting hemp.
Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} or {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} entered the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|en|Amano-Iwato|WD=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|en|Futodama|WD=Q11059539|lt=Futodama}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=asa}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|WD=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.
== References == <references/>

Also called '''Oegami''' ('''麻植神,''' おえのかみ) and '''Imbe no Kami''' ('''忌部神,''' いんべのかみ) <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>. Additionally, known as '''Amanohiwashikakeru Yano Mikoto''' ('''天日鷲翔矢命,''' あめのひわしかけるやのみこと), a descendant deity of {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi}} or {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901|WD=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi}}.
== Overview == According to the mythology, when {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} entered the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|en|Amano-Iwato|WD=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}}, the gods began dancing in front of the rock door, and Amanohiwashi no Kami played a stringed instrument. When a hawk landed on the tip of the string, many gods took it as an auspicious sign to brighten the world, and the deity's name was augmented with the character for hawk, becoming Amanohiwashi no Mikoto. Later, {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}} mentioned that the hawk was the same bird that contributed to Emperor Jimmu's victory in battle <ref name="weekly_shrine">{{Cite book|和書|author=|title=週刊日本の神社 No67 忌部神社 大麻比古神社 |publisher=デアゴスティーニ・ジャパン|date=2015-5-28|page=14-19}}</ref>.
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, it is written that in one account of the Amanoiwato, "Amanohiwashi no Mikoto of Awa's Imbe, the distant ancestor, made {{ill|hemp|ja|木綿 (ゆう)|translation=wooden strips|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|1=hemp|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519320}}."
According to the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|WD=Q531555|lt=Kojiki}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is one of the four deities who followed {{ill|Futodama|en|Futodama|WD=Q11059539|lt=Futodama}}. Also, when {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} hid in the Amanoiwato, it is said that Amanohiwashi no Kami planted hemp and mulberry to make the offerings. Therefore, Amanohiwashi no Kami is also called "Moegami" (おえのかみ) and became the deity of the spinning and paper-making industries. Additionally, {{ill|Amatomi no Mikoto|ja|天富命|translation=Amatomi no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanotomohime|qq=}} led the grandchildren of Amanohiwashi no Kami to Awa and planted hemp and {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=asa}}.
In the "Kokuzou Honki" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, it is said that in the era of Emperor Jimmu, Amanohiwashi no Mikoto was appointed as the Ise Kokuzou. Meanwhile, in the {{ill|Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja|伊勢国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'s remaining passages, it is written that Emperor Jimmu sent Amanohiwashi no Mikoto to Ise, where he subdued Isetsuhiko.
Additionally, Amanohiwashi no Kami is generally known as {{ill|Otori-sama|ja|お酉様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otori-sama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 酉の市}} at {{ill|Ōtori Shrine|ja|大鷲神社|lt=Ōtori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori Shrine|qq=}}, and is worshipped as the guardian deity of bountiful fishing, commercial prosperity, good luck, pioneering, and industry.
Amanohiwashi no Kami is enshrined at {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|WD=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine}} and {{ill|Tori Shrine|ja|鷲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tori Shrine|1=Washi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11676283}}. Imbe Shrine is associated with the Mikake family, who are believed to be descendants of the Awa Imbe and grow hemp for the emperor's enthronement ritual <ref name="weekly_shrine"/>.
According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, Amanohiwashi no Kami is considered an ancestor of the Inukai clan and Amakoto no Muraji.
